attributes.description: Alerts when the ALB rejects connections because it reached its connection ceiling. A hard capacity-class failure requiring immediate attention.
attributes.name: [AWS ELB OTel] Application Load Balancer rejected connections detected

investigation_guide.blob:
## Rejected Connections Detected

Non-zero `RejectedConnectionCount` means the ALB dropped incoming connections because it hit its connection limit. Clients experience connection failures.

### Triage Steps
1. Identify the affected load balancer and region.
2. Check `ActiveConnectionCount` and `NewConnectionCount` for connection storm patterns.
3. Review `ConsumedLCUs` — the LB may be approaching capacity limits.
4. Look for connection leaks on clients (missing keep-alive, retry storms) or DDoS/abuse traffic.
5. Consider requesting an ALB pre-warming or capacity review from AWS if sustained.

### Escalation
Any rejected connections on a production ALB warrant immediate investigation.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.elb.otel-*
| WHERE Namespace == "AWS/ApplicationELB"
    AND MetricName == "RejectedConnectionCount"
    AND stat == "Sum"
| STATS rejected = SUM(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ApplicationELB/RejectedConnectionCount`)
    BY LoadBalancer, cloud.region
// Any rejected connection is notable — threshold intentionally 0
| WHERE rejected > 0
| SORT rejected DESC
